name: Full-width explicit PTX load-order follow-through without half-panel compute

summary.name: Full-width explicit PTX load-order follow-through without half-panel compute
summary.hypothesis: With barrier at 14.15 and mio at 2.32, the branch no longer looks primarily orchestration-bound. Long scoreboard is now the larger visible stall and compute-memory/DRAM throughput are already in the mid-50% range. A coherent next step inside the same 64x384 PTX branch is to push compute/load control further by replacing the remaining wrapper-style wmma.load/wmma.mma surface with a full-width explicit PTX dataflow, while explicitly avoiding the prechecked explicit mma.sync half-panel subpath that regressed. The point is to improve load order and fragment residency on the intact 12-tile hot band, not to split the tile into a separate 192-column compute path.
summary.expected_bottleneck: Residual load-to-use latency and fragment feed ordering inside the PTX compute body, now that barrier and mio overhead are largely under control.
summary.risk: This has more upside than dir_01 but is materially riskier in the last round. If the load-order rewrite drifts toward the old half-panel explicit mma.sync experiment, it can easily give back the steady progress from the last two rounds.
summary.stop_condition: Stop if the implementation starts serializing the hot band into the already-rejected half-panel path, or if long-scoreboard and runtime do not improve together.

notes: Build passed. Node A must measure this implementation next.
